root_causes.cause: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively shutdown, causing the link to be down and R1's Serial0/1/0 to report oper=lower-layer-down (while admin up).
evidence: R2 running-config: Serial0/1/0 contains "shutdown" (get_running_config for R2).
R2 interface status: admin_state="down", oper_state="down", last_change="2026-05-26T09:37:34..." (get_interface_status for R2 Serial0/1/0).
R1 interface status: admin_state="up", oper_state="lower-layer-down", last_change="2026-05-26T09:37:37..." (get_interface_status for R1 Serial0/1/0).
R1/R2 OSPF summaries: Serial0/1/0 listed as interface_state="Down" with no neighbors on both routers, while other serial links to R3 show FULL neighbors (get_ospf outputs).
Topology/config evidence: interface IPs and descriptions show R1 Serial0/1/0 <-> R2 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.1/30 <-> 10.0.0.2/30), confirming the affected link (running-config).

root_causes.cause: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively shutdown, causing the link to be down and R1's Serial0/1/0 to report oper=lower-layer-down (while admin up).
evidence: R2 running-config: Serial0/1/0 contains "shutdown" (get_running_config for R2).
R2 interface status: admin_state="down", oper_state="down", last_change="2026-05-26T09:37:34..." (get_interface_status for R2 Serial0/1/0).
R1 interface status: admin_state="up", oper_state="lower-layer-down", last_change="2026-05-26T09:37:37..." (get_interface_status for R1 Serial0/1/0).
R1/R2 OSPF summaries: Serial0/1/0 listed as interface_state="Down" with no neighbors on both routers, while other serial links to R3 show FULL neighbors (get_ospf outputs).
Topology/config evidence: interface IPs and descriptions show R1 Serial0/1/0 <-> R2 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.1/30 <-> 10.0.0.2/30), confirming the affected link (running-config).

data.intent_description: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively down, causing the R1<->R2 serial link to be down and OSPF adjacency loss. Restore the physical/link connectivity by enabling R2 Serial0/1/0 so the interface reaches oper up and OSPF neighbors return.

plan.problem: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively shutdown, causing the link to R1 to be down and OSPF neighbor loss.
plan.fix_summary: Bring R2 Serial0/1/0 admin state to up (no shutdown) to restore the link and OSPF adjacency.

root_causes.cause: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively shutdown, causing the link to be down and R1's Serial0/1/0 to report oper=lower-layer-down (while admin up).
evidence: R2 running-config: Serial0/1/0 contains "shutdown" (get_running_config for R2).
R2 interface status: admin_state="down", oper_state="down", last_change="2026-05-26T09:37:34..." (get_interface_status for R2 Serial0/1/0).
R1 interface status: admin_state="up", oper_state="lower-layer-down", last_change="2026-05-26T09:37:37..." (get_interface_status for R1 Serial0/1/0).
R1/R2 OSPF summaries: Serial0/1/0 listed as interface_state="Down" with no neighbors on both routers, while other serial links to R3 show FULL neighbors (get_ospf outputs).
Topology/config evidence: interface IPs and descriptions show R1 Serial0/1/0 <-> R2 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.1/30 <-> 10.0.0.2/30), confirming the affected link (running-config).

plan.problem: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively shutdown, causing the link to R1 to be down and OSPF neighbor loss.
plan.fix_summary: Bring R2 Serial0/1/0 admin state to up (no shutdown) to restore the link and OSPF adjacency.

messages.content:
What was observed
- R1 Serial0/1/0: admin=up, oper=lower-layer-down. R2 Serial0/1/0: admin=down, oper=down. OSPF adjacency between R1↔R2 was down (other R1/R2↔R3 adjacencies were healthy). IPs: R1 10.0.0.1/30 ↔ R2 10.0.0.2/30.

Diagnosis (root cause)
- R2 Serial0/1/0 was administratively shutdown, causing the physical/link outage and OSPF neighbor loss.

Changes made
- Enabled R2 Serial0/1/0 (no shutdown).

Verification
- Passed.
  - R2 Serial0/1/0: admin=up, oper=up.
  - R1 Serial0/1/0: admin=up, oper=up.
  - OSPF adjacency restored: R1 shows neighbor 2.2.2.2 (10.0.0.2) state FULL; R2 shows neighbor 1.1.1.1 (10.0.0.1) state FULL.

Remaining items
- None.

Verification

  - R2 Serial0/1/0: admin=up, oper=up.
  - R1 Serial0/1/0: admin=up, oper=up.
  - OSPF adjacency restored: R1 shows neighbor 2.2.2.2 (10.0.0.2) state FULL; R2 shows neighbor 1.1.1.1 (10.0.0.1) state FULL.
